title: Report
description: One settled snapshot of the machine.

battery.description: Battery and adapter state. `null` on machines without a battery.

cpu.description: CPU utilization and load. `null` if the fast tier did not settle.

disk.description: Disk I/O, capacity, and filesystems. `null` if the block-storage source is down.

flows.description: Per-connection network flows. `null` if the ntstat source is down.

gpu.description: GPU utilization and memory. `null` if the AGX source is down.

kernel.description: Kernel activity: scheduler rates, interrupts, and sleep blockers.

memory.description: System memory and swap. `null` if the memory source is down.

meta.description:
Provenance: contract version, capture time, settle state, and which
optional collectors ran.

network.description: Network throughput and the primary interface. `null` if the source is down.

power.description: Power rails and CPU/GPU frequency. `null` if IOReport is unavailable.

processes.description: Process-table summary and top consumers. `null` if the process pass is down.

soc.description: Immutable machine facts. Never null: loaded synchronously at startup.

source_errors.description:
One entry per collector that failed at startup. Always present, often
empty. A key elsewhere being `null` with a matching entry here means
"the source is down", as opposed to "disabled" or "not yet settled".

storage.description: Drive health (SMART, cache, throttle). `null` when `storage_health` is off.

thermal.description: Temperatures, fans, and thermal pressure. `null` if the SMC sweep is down.

battery_w.description: Signed: positive while charging, negative while discharging.

cell_imbalance_mv.description:
Spread between the highest and lowest cell, the earliest sign of a
failing pack.

cycle_count.description: Charge cycles the pack has been through.

cycle_ratio.description: Cycles used as a fraction of the pack's rated cycle life.

daily_soc.description: The optimized-charging band the pack has lived in recently.

design_cycles.description: Cycles the pack is rated for.

fully_charged.description: Whether the OS considers the pack full.

health_ratio.description: Current full-charge capacity vs design capacity, 0..1.

minutes_remaining.description: Minutes to full (charging) or empty (discharging), when the OS knows.

not_charging_reason.description: Non-zero reason code when on AC but not charging.

thermally_limited_secs.description: Seconds of charging the pack has spent thermally limited.

Cluster.description: A CPU cluster's aggregate readings plus its per-core breakdown.

cores.description: Per-core breakdown, sorted by (die, cluster, core).

freq_mhz.description: Residency-weighted average frequency across the cluster.

usage_ratio.description: Average effective utilization across the cluster, 0..1.

nand_written_bytes.description: Bytes the controller moved to flash over the window.

throttled_ratio.description: Share of the window the drive spent thermally throttled.

Core.description:
One core's frequency, usage, and (when the chip publishes a per-core rail)
power.

power_w.description:
The core's own energy rail in watts. `null` on chips that publish no
per-core rail: absent power is not zero power.

Cpu.description:
CPU utilization and system load. Per-core frequency and power live under
[`Power`] (a different source: IOReport, not mach).

per_core_ratio.description:
Busy fraction per logical core, 0..1, in kernel order (E-cluster first).
Empty if the per-core read failed.

self_ratio.description: mxmon's own CPU use, as a fraction of one core.

DailySoc.description: The optimized-charging state-of-charge band, as ratios.

max_ratio.description: High end of the optimized-charging band, 0..1.

min_ratio.description: Low end of the optimized-charging band, 0..1.

Disk.description: Disk throughput, latency, capacity, and mounted filesystems.

capacity_available_bytes.description: Free space on the data volume.

capacity_total_bytes.description: Capacity of the data volume the system booted from.

capacity_used_ratio.description: Used fraction of the data volume, 0..1.

filesystems.description: Every real, sized filesystem currently mounted.

read_latency_us.description: Average per-op read latency, when any op has been seen.

read_session_bytes.description: Read bytes accumulated since mxmon launched.

write_latency_us.description: Average per-op write latency, when any op has been seen.

write_session_bytes.description: Write bytes accumulated since mxmon launched.

Fan.description: One fan's current and maximum speed.

ratio.description: Current speed as a fraction of maximum, when the maximum is known.

Features.description: The optional collectors and whether each was enabled.

storage_health.description: NVMe SMART, APFS cache, and controller-throttle polling.

retransmit_ratio.description: Lifetime retransmitted share of transmitted bytes, 0..1.

rtt_ms.description: Smoothed round-trip time; `null` for UDP or before any measurement.

rx_total_bytes.description: Bytes received over the connection's life.

state.description: TCP state name, or empty for UDP.

tx_total_bytes.description: Bytes sent over the connection's life.

udp.description: True for UDP, false for TCP.

count.description: All live sources, including ones not shown in `top`.

rx_bytes_per_sec.description: Total receive rate across all connections.

top.description: Busiest connections, most-active first, capped at 10.

tx_bytes_per_sec.description: Total transmit rate across all connections.

Gpu.description:
GPU utilization and memory. GPU frequency and active ratio live under
[`Power`] (the IOReport source).

device_ratio.description: Overall device utilization, 0..1 (matches Activity Monitor's GPU meter).

used_memory_bytes.description: In-use system memory attributed to the GPU.

handler_cpu_ratio.description: Handler time as a share of the window (the part that costs CPU).

per_sec.description: Interrupts per second from this device.

Interrupts.description: Interrupt activity, aggregated to the busiest sources.

total_per_sec.description: Total interrupts per second across all sources.

Kernel.description:
Kernel activity: scheduler rates (from the process pass), interrupt sources,
and sleep blockers (from the `kernel_stats` collector).

rates.description: System-wide scheduler rates. `null` if the process pass has not settled.

sleep_blockers.description:
What is currently holding the machine awake. `null` when `kernel_stats`
is off.

Memory.description: System memory (the Activity Monitor formula), swap, and pressure.

cached_bytes.description: File-backed and purgeable pages the OS can reclaim.

Meta.description: Provenance for the snapshot: how to read it and how fresh it is.

features.description:
Which optional collectors were enabled for this run. Disambiguates a
`null` domain that is "disabled" from one that is "down" or "unsettled".

generated_unix.description: Wall-clock capture time, seconds since the Unix epoch.

mxmon_version.description: The mxmon build that produced this report.

sample_window.description: Delta windows the printed rates average over, per tier, in milliseconds.

schema_version.description: Contract version. Bumped on any breaking schema change. Currently 1.

settled.description:
True iff every settle-gated tier reported at least twice before the
deadline. When false, some fresh-but-single-sample or absent values may
be present, and delta-based rates may still be zero.

Network.description: Network throughput and the primary interface.

primary.description: The busiest active interface, when one is up.

rx_bytes_per_sec.description: Receive throughput, aggregated over physical interfaces.

rx_session_bytes.description: Received bytes accumulated since mxmon launched.

tx_bytes_per_sec.description: Transmit throughput, aggregated over physical interfaces.

tx_session_bytes.description: Transmitted bytes accumulated since mxmon launched.

rtt_ms.description: This probe's round trip; `null` on timeout.

Power.description: Power rails and CPU/GPU frequency from IOReport energy counters.

amcc_w.description: Memory-controller fabric (`AMCC`), separate from the DRAM rail.

display_ext_w.description: External display pipeline (the external share of `display_w`).

ecpu.description: Efficiency cluster (the "E" tier; the mid tier on M5 Pro/Max).

gpu_active_ratio.description: Fraction of the window the GPU was powered on.

package_w.description: Package power: CPU + GPU + ANE rails.

pcpu.description: Performance cluster (the "P" tier; "S" on M5 Pro/Max).

link_speed_bps.description: Link speed in bits per second (a bit-rate by definition).

link_up.description: Link actually up (`IFF_RUNNING`), not merely configured.

Proc.description: One process, enriched where permissions allowed.

cpu_ratio.description: CPU as a fraction of one core; can exceed 1 for multithreaded work.

p_share_ratio.description: Fraction of the window's cycles spent on P-cluster cores.

power_w.description: Average power over the window, from per-process energy counters.

qos_background_ratio.description: Share of CPU time requested at background QoS, 0..1.

qos_interactive_ratio.description: Share of CPU time requested at interactive QoS, 0..1.

runnable.description: Seconds runnable-but-not-running per wall second (scheduler contention).

Processes.description: The process table summary and the top consumers by CPU.

restricted.description: True when at least one process was unreadable.

running.description: Processes currently running (not sleeping or idle).

threads_visible.description: Threads across readable processes (a lower bound without sudo).

top.description: Busiest processes by CPU, most-active first, capped at 12.

fast_ms.description: Fast tier: cpu, gpu, memory, network, disk.

SleepBlocker.description: One reason the machine is being held awake.

reason.description: Human-readable reason the owner supplied, when any.

Smart.description: The NVMe SMART / Health Information log page.

available_spare_threshold_ratio.description: Threshold below which the drive considers itself failing, 0..1.

bytes_read.description:
Bytes read over the drive's life. Narrowed from the log's 128-bit
counter to u64 (saturating); values past 2^53 lose precision in JSON
parsers that use doubles.

bytes_written.description: Bytes written over the drive's life (same u64 narrowing as `bytes_read`).

critical_warning.description: Fault bit field; any bit set is a controller-reported problem.

error_log_entries.description: Entries in the controller error log.

power_cycles.description: Power-cycle count over the drive's life.

power_on_hours.description: Powered-on hours over the drive's life.

unhealthy.description: True when the controller is flagging a real problem.

unsafe_shutdowns.description: Power losses without a clean shutdown.

cores_per_pcluster.description: Cores per performance cluster (Max parts run more than one cluster).

ecpu_freqs_mhz.description: E-tier DVFS steps, ascending, in MHz.

gpu_cores.description: GPU core count, when the registry publishes it.

gpu_freqs_mhz.description: GPU DVFS steps, ascending, in MHz.

pcpu_freqs_mhz.description: P-tier DVFS steps, ascending, in MHz.

tier_high.description: Display letter for the upper CPU tier ("P" on M1-M4, "S" on M5 Pro/Max).

tier_low.description: Display letter for the lower CPU tier ("E" on M1-M4, "P" on M5 Pro/Max).

SourceError.description: A collector that failed at startup, with the reason.

source.description: The collector that failed (matches a top-level domain key).

Storage.description:
Drive health: NVMe SMART, APFS per-volume cache behaviour, and controller
throttle counters. The health tier; `null` when `storage_health` is off.

smart.description: The NVMe SMART log page, when the drive exposes it.

volumes.description:
APFS per-volume cache and write behaviour (distinct from
[`Disk::filesystems`], which is capacity).

Thermal.description: Temperatures, fans, the kernel's thermal-pressure verdict, and SMC power.

adapter_power_w.description: SMC watts delivered by the adapter right now (`PDTR`).

pressure.description:
Kernel thermal-pressure level: "nominal" through "sleeping". `null` if
the notification key is unavailable.

sensors.description: Every readable die and board sensor.

severity.description: Pressure severity on a 0..1 ramp, for gauges.

throttling.description:
Whether the OS considers the machine thermally constrained (pressure at
"moderate" or above).

Volume.description: One APFS volume's cache and write behaviour.

cache_hit_ratio.description:
Share of reads served without touching the device, when anything was
read.

device_read_bytes.description: Bytes that actually reached the device (the gap is the cache).

device_write_bytes.description: Bytes that actually reached the device.

write_amplification.description: Device bytes written per byte userspace wrote.
